---
title: "2. Understanding R"
author: "Sam McAllister"
format: 
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    toc-title: Contents
    code-overflow: wrap
editor: visual
---

## 1. Open RStudio

If on a **Mac**, use the Finder to locate the **RStudio** application.

If on **Windows**, you can use the search bar at the bottom and look for **RStudio**.

Even though we install R, **we do not need to open this**. RStudio works off of R, so we need it installed, but it knows where everything is and uses it in the background.

## 2. Open a new script

At the top of RStudio, press **File \> New File \> R Script**.

The R script is what we write code on, which we then run parts of the script or code to take an effect.

When you have opened the R script, you will notice that there are 4 panels to work from:

-   **Source** - top left, this lets you work on a script and save it, necessary for writing code!

-   **Environment** - top right, will tell you what data exists in your session.

-   **Console** - bottom left, this is a simplified version of the command line on R, whenever we run code from the script we will see it running down here

-   **Plots, Help, Packages** - bottom right, where we will see plots, where we can look for help in RStudio, and see what packages are already loaded.

![](https://docs.posit.co/ide/user/ide/get-started/images/rstudio-panes-labeled.jpeg)

## 3. Create a folder to store data

Outside of RStudio, create a folder to store the data you'll work with in this tutorial.

You might want to name it "**MMI_Lab_1**".

If you haven't already, download the UK Demographic dataset on [Moodle](https://moodle.gla.ac.uk/mod/book/view.php?id=5162592), and store it in the MMI_Lab_1 folder. This will be the **UK_Demographic.csv** file.

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
## Note

A .csv file is one that contains "comma-separated values".

Think of it like Excel, instead of each value being in a cell, they are separated by commas. You can open it with a programme like "Notepad" to see what the format really looks like.

If you open this file in Excel, DO NOT SAVE IT, as the hidden format will change and it will be difficult to load in R.
:::

## 4. Change the working directory

A working directory is the location R is currently working in, so we can load or save data in this directory. You'll see the current working directory, as below, in the **console**.

![](images/clipboard-3314005215.png)

You can change the working directory by clicking on the Session \> Set Working Directory \> Choose Directory.

Set the working directory to the MMI_Labs_1 folder that you created.

## 5. Save and work from the Rscript

To save the script, hit **File** \> **Save As**.

Maybe give it a memorable name, like MMI_Lab_1.R.
